key_insight: Waterproof, an educational proof assistant, appears to help first-year students write more explicit and transferable proofs in an introductory proof course.
engineering_relevance: This suggests proof-assistance tools can improve mathematical reasoning workflows and support scalable proof education across mixed-language, multi-instructor settings.
method: Quasi-experiment in an introductory proof course
Observed students in class and collected survey responses
Analyzed student performance and proof structure across contexts
evidence_signal: The abstract reports carryover to pen-and-paper proofs and higher grades for Mathematics-Computer Science students using Waterproof, based on observational and performance data.
watch_item: Because students self-selected into using Waterproof, the findings are suggestive rather than causal.
paper_title: The Educational Proof Assistant Waterproof in an Introductory Proof Course: Proof Construction and Learning Processes
